# Transcrição: 08- Renting a Car in Boca Raton

- **Fonte:** 08- Renting a Car in Boca Raton
- **Duração:** 07:08
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:00]

I'm standing next to my wife or actually my fiancee at the time, Alicia. We're in the car rental facility adjacent to Boca Raton Airport. I watch as Alicia passes her driver's license across to the agent on the other side and I watch as the agent, her smile turns to a frown as she looks at the driver's license and she says, I'm sorry, I can't rent a car to you. Your license is expired and my heart soars. I am so thrilled by this news. I've known Alicia for three years. We've been together for 18 months and we've been engaged for about a year. So we're still at that point in the relationship where we haven't discovered each other's ugliness yet. We haven't turned over all the rocks to see like what's underneath. I have yet to discover that Alicia has a disturbing love for Steely Dan. I have yet to learn that she can walk into a room and put a bin or a box or a bag down in that room and then just forget that it ever exists. Not that she ignores it, it just becomes part of the topography of the room and it will never move again if I do not move that thing. When we move for three months from now, her mess is so extraordinary that we take sheets and we lie on the floor and we throw her stuff on the sheets and tie the corners like giant hobo luggage and we just drag it out of the apartment. But all of that pales in comparison to the ugliness that she is not yet seen from me, my under the rock awfulness. She doesn't realize that she's about to marry someone who is completely useless in almost every way. I have never made a hammer connect to a nail in any effective way. I can't paint a room. I can't replace a hinge. When her car breaks down, I throw the hood up and I scrutinize the engine. But in my head, I'm thinking, well, there's windshield wiper fluid, so it's not that. And that's all I know. That is the extent of my engine knowledge. She doesn't know that 12 years from now, I will hand my friend a lamp from my daughter's bedroom and say, this lamp is broken. Can you fix it? And he will return it one day later and say, I replaced the bulb. And then one year later, I will hand that same lamp to that same man and say, this time it's really broken. And he will hand it back to me one day later and say, I replaced the bulb again. These hands do not build and repair. They purchase and replace. And this is what she is in for. And yet I have one skill and it's this one here. Now I am organized. I am a pedantic asshole and nothing gets by me. I miss no deadlines. I have file cabinets inside of file cabinets and I sure as hell don't have a driver's license that is expired in my wallet. And this is how she's going to be saved by me.

## [03:00 - 05:59]

So I say to her, don't worry. And I hand my driver's license over the agent. We make this eye contact. We have this silent conversation. She's thinking like, are you sure you want to marry this savage? And I look back at her and my eyes tell her, it's fine. I'm doing the Lord's work. So I give her my driver's license because I am a hero and she takes it and she starts processing and then she looks at my license and she says, your driver's license is expired too. And that is not possible because I am not the kind of person who walks around with an expired driver's license, but it is true. Two days before I had my birthday and I let my driver's license expire. And this is a disaster because we're in Boca to visit Nana and that means Nana's going to pick us up at the airport. And that means she is in charge of our week, which means it is not going to be a good week. But there's four more rental agencies. There's Enterprise and Hertz and at the end there's Alamo. So we have a shot. And so one by one, we go to each one of these rental counters and we plead and we beg to rent a car and all of them say no. And the last one is Alamo and it's not lost on me that my last stand will be at the Alamo. And I see that there's a guy there. He's wearing a suit, but he's got a Philadelphia Eagles jersey over it. And I turn to Alicia and for reasons I will never understand, I say, I'm going to get you this car. You stay right here. And then I turn around and think, what the hell are you thinking? When I approach him, I'm thinking I'm going to pretend to be an Eagles fan because they had just played the Patriots in the Super Bowl. So I know the team. But as I get close, it just doesn't feel right. And so I go with the truth instead. I go up and I say, I'd like to rent a car. And he says, great. And I say, by the way, I'm a Patriots fan. I'm so sorry. What happened to your team in the Super Bowl? And he says, yeah, it was lousy. And I say, you had the best team other than my team. I said, you were the team we didn't want to face. And he's saying, yeah, it was rotten. And I said, Donovan McNabb is one of the best quarterbacks. If you just had someone to like stand up in front of him and like take care of him. And he's like, yeah, no drama receiver, someone who wouldn't complain and would just catch the ball. I say, yeah, you're right. And then I say, all right, I'd like to rent the car and I handed my driver's license and I just wait and I'm watching him and his smile turns to a frown as expected. And he looks up at me and he says, I'm sorry, man, I can't rent you a car. Your license has expired. And I pretend to be surprised. And I lean in and I say, listen, do you see that girl over there? I said, she's my fiance, but I am holding on by a thread, man. Like, I don't know if it's going to happen. And he says, there's nothing I can do for you. And I said, have you ever been in one of those situations where you just wish someone would stand up for you, protect you, like take care of you, like lead you to the promised land? And he laughs because he knows what I'm doing. He's not stupid. He understands exactly what my plan was and what I am doing now.

## [06:00 - 07:08]

But he agrees to rent me the car. And so I get the paperwork and I walk back to Alicia and I say, I got that car. And she says, how the hell did you do that? And I say, I took care of you, honey. We have been together for 13 years. I have never in all of those 13 years made a hammer connect to a nail. And anyway, we just paid thousands of dollars to have our entire house painted on the inside work that most people do on their own. That I cannot do. I can't fix an engine. I can't replace a hinge. She replaced a hinge on a cabin in our kitchen this week. I still don't know how the fuck she did it. It's astounding to me. But the one thing I do is I can talk. It's why I write books and what's why I stand on stages and tell stories. And it's how I get through with all of the things I need to get through. It's not building and it's not repairing. It's none of those great things. It's a little thing, but it is enough to hold on to my wife by a thread. Thank you.
